title: EIP-7885: Precompile for NTT operations
description: Adds four precompiled contracts for Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) operations: forward NTT, inverse NTT, vectorized modular multiplication, and vectorized modular addition. These enable efficient polynomial multiplication needed for post-quantum signature verification (Falcon, Dilithium) and STARK proof verification, reducing gas costs by over 70% compared to pure Solidity implementations.
author: Renaud Dubois (@rdubois-crypto), Simon Masson (@simonmasson), Yoon Hyoung Lee (@yhl125)
laymanDescription: Adds built-in math operations to Ethereum that make it dramatically cheaper to verify post-quantum cryptographic signatures on-chain. These same operations also speed up zero-knowledge proof verification. This prepares Ethereum for a future where quantum computers could break current signature schemes.

endUsers.description: No immediate change. In the future, enables quantum-resistant account signatures at practical gas costs, protecting user funds against quantum attacks.

appDevs.description: Smart contract developers gain access to efficient polynomial arithmetic via four new precompiles, enabling post-quantum signature verification and faster STARK proof checks.

walletDevs.description: No immediate impact. Eventually enables wallets to support post-quantum signature schemes like Falcon and Dilithium for transaction signing.

toolingInfra.description: Development tools and testing frameworks must support the four new precompile addresses and their input/output encoding formats.

layer2s.description: L2s using STARK-based proofs benefit from cheaper on-chain verification. The precompiles support fields used by Risc0, Plonky2, and Circle STARKs.

stakersNodes.description: No direct impact on validators or staking operations. Node operators must upgrade clients to include the new precompile implementations.

elClients.description: Must implement four new precompiled contracts with NTT algorithms, scheme-specific gas metering, and input validation for multiple cryptographic parameter sets.

clClients.description: No consensus layer changes required. This EIP only adds execution layer precompiles.

benefits: Reduces post-quantum signature verification gas costs by over 70% compared to pure Solidity.
Provides cryptographic agility, supporting Falcon, Dilithium, Kyber, and multiple STARK fields.
Accelerates on-chain STARK proof verification through efficient polynomial multiplication.
Prepares Ethereum for quantum computing threats without committing to a single algorithm.
tradeoffs: Adds four new precompiles, increasing EL client implementation and maintenance burden.
Gas cost model varies by cryptographic scheme, adding complexity to gas estimation.

scaleL1.description: Speeds up on-chain STARK proof verification by providing efficient NTT operations over fields used by major proof systems (Risc0, Plonky2, Circle STARKs).

improveUX.description: Enables practical post-quantum signature verification on L1, protecting users against future quantum computing threats at reasonable gas costs.
